Brand.title: Brand
Brand.description: Represents a brand of auto parts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Brand entity.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category of auto parts (e.g., Engine, Suspension, Body).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.
Unique identifier for the Part entity.

sku.description: The stock keeping unit code for the part.

stock.description: The number of parts in stock.

imageUrls.description: URLs of the images for the part.

categoryIds.description: References to Categories. (Relationship: Category N:N Part)

vehicleBrandId.description: The brand of the vehicle this part is for.

vehicleModelId.description: The model of the vehicle this part is for.

admins.title: Admin User
admins.description: An empty document whose ID is the UID of a Firebase Auth user. The existence of this document indicates that the user has admin privileges. No properties are stored in the document itself.

definition.description: Stores brand information. Each document represents a brand of auto parts.
Stores category information. Each document represents a category of auto parts.
Stores auto part information. Each document represents an auto part.
Collection of admin users. The presence of a document indicates admin privileges.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to support the Auto Parts Hub application, focusing on a clean separation of concerns, security, and efficient data retrieval. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and supports listing operations (QAPs) through structural segregation. Brands, Categories, and Parts are stored in separate collections at the root level to facilitate easy management and querying. The administrative functionality is secured through a dedicated 'admins' collection, leveraging the principle of 'Existence over Content' for role management, enhancing security. The structure uses denormalization strategically to avoid complex security rules and maintain data integrity.
